user_message: This just came in from David Rivera at Fourth Coffee. Can you identify the blockers?

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-20 12:00 EST
Subject: Re: Re: executive brief Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for executive brief. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 15:22 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 13:56 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

Our team encountered some challenges with executive brief. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-03-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), David (Fourth Coffee)

[00:02:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:06:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:11:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:26:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:31:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:36:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:38:00] David (Fourth Coffee): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:41:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fourth Coffee: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF14-F trap (Skips technical-decision-maker role); produces complete Stakeholder map with roles (decision-maker, influencer, champion, blocker), recency labels, confiden
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF14-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF14-F failure mode (Skips technical-decision-maker role)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF14-F -- Skips technical-decision-maker role
